snapshot: Japan's cash equity market is closed for Showa Day. JPX's holiday calendar lists April 29, 2026 as Showa Day.
Nikkei 225 night-session futures ended at 59,010 and TOPIX futures at 3,733.5, implying a sizable downside gap versus the prior cash close.
Reuters reported that Japan was closed for a holiday, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan was down 0.2%, Brent was at $111.71, and S&P 500 futures were slightly higher.
USD/JPY was around 159.55, keeping intervention risk near the 160 level alive. The FOMC and U.S. mega-cap tech earnings are the next key external checks.
After the April 28 close, large buybacks from Fujitsu, Shin-Etsu Chemical and JR Central were announced, but cash-market price discovery is delayed until April 30.

drivers.driver: No same-day cash price discovery because Japan is closed
drivers.why_today: Japan cash equities are shut for Showa Day, so there is no same-day order book, turnover or sector-relative tape to validate a midday long ranking.

drivers.driver: External conditions remain unstable into the holiday
drivers.why_today: Reuters highlighted U.S. equity weakness, AI-related concerns, a stalled Iran process and the pending FOMC decision. Those inputs can still reset the next cash session.
Fujitsu, Shin-Etsu Chemical, JR Central and Fuji Electric all announced meaningful buybacks after the prior close, but the holiday means the cash market has not processed them yet.

headline_inventory.headline: JPX's holiday calendar lists April 29, 2026 as Showa Day, meaning Japan's domestic market is closed
Kabutan's economic schedule also identifies April 29 as a domestic market holiday
Nikkei 225 night-session futures ended at 59,010 and TOPIX futures at 3,733.5
Reuters reported that while Japan was closed, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan fell 0.2%, Brent reached $111.71, and S&P 500 futures edged higher
Reuters reported USD/JPY around 159.55, with intervention risk still active near the 160 level
Shin-Etsu Chemical announced a buyback capped at 2.42% of shares outstanding and 250 billion yen
Fujitsu announced a buyback capped at 5.76% of shares outstanding and 150 billion yen
JR Central announced a buyback capped at 0.68% of shares outstanding and 20 billion yen, with acquired shares to be cancelled
Fuji Electric announced a buyback capped at 1.7% of shares outstanding and 21 billion yen
Chuo Seisakusho disclosed revisions to FY March 2026 earnings guidance and its dividend forecast
Ryoyu Systems guided for a fifth straight record profit, raised the prior-year dividend and signaled a real dividend increase for the current year

deep_dive.title: Showa Day holiday edition: no watchlist today, only a carry-over map into the next cash session

lines: April 29 is Showa Day, so Japan's cash market is closed and there is no live order book, turnover or sector-relative tape to validate stock selection.
For this midday edition, the long watchlist is intentionally empty and the focus is on external inputs and unprocessed after-close disclosures for April 30.
Both JPX and Kabutan confirm the domestic market holiday, while futures, FX, crude and U.S. equity-related inputs have continued to move.
Nikkei 225 futures ended the night session at 59,010, materially below the prior cash close, while U.S. AI concerns and Iran headlines remain relevant for the next cash open.
Into the April 30 open, the first question is whether Brent around $111, USD/JPY in the mid-159s and Nikkei futures near 59,010 hold or reprice.
The second is whether large buybacks from Fujitsu, Shin-Etsu Chemical, JR Central and Fuji Electric can offset index pressure as stock-specific catalysts.
FOMC, U.S. mega-cap tech earnings or Iran-related headlines could materially change the setup while Japan is closed.
Holiday futures and FX moves should not be mapped mechanically onto individual cash equities without fresh price discovery.
A holiday edition has no same-day cash price discovery, so ranking precision is intentionally reduced.
Additional disclosures or overseas events before the April 30 open can change the priority of April 28 after-close materials.

no_trade_conditions: When the domestic cash market is closed and there is no same-day price discovery to validate stock selection
When holiday moves in futures, FX or crude are being copied mechanically into individual cash-equity ideas
When the FOMC, U.S. mega-cap tech earnings or Iran-related headlines materially alter the next-session premise
When April 28 after-close TDnet filings are altered in substance by follow-up disclosure or company clarification before the next cash session
